an4.182:1.1:
1
"Bhikkhus, against four things there can be no guarantor, neither an ascetic, a brahmin, a deva, Māra, Brahmā, nor anyone in the world.
an4.182:1.2:
2
(1) What four? "There can be no guarantor, neither an ascetic … nor anyone in the world, that what is subject to old age will not grow old.
an4.182:1.3: (2) "There can be no guarantor, neither an ascetic … nor any one in the world, that what is subject to illness will not fall ill.
an4.182:1.4: (3) "There can be no guarantor, neither an ascetic … nor anyone in the world, that what is subject to death will not die.
an4.182:1.5: (4) "There can be no guarantor, neither an ascetic … nor anyone in the world, that bad kamma—defiled, conducive to renewed existence, troublesome, ripening in suffering, leading to future birth, old age, and death—will not produce its result.
an4.182:2.2:
3
"Against these four things, bhikkhus, there can be no guarantor, neither an ascetic, a brahmin, a deva, Māra, Brahmā, nor anyone in the world."
